description: BlackRock is a global asset manager whose core business spans investment funds, ETFs, portfolio management, risk analytics, advisory services, and institutional investment technology.

overview.title: Business Scope
paragraphs: BlackRock is one of the world’s largest investment managers, serving institutions, financial advisors, governments, and individual investors through public-market funds, private-market strategies, advisory mandates, and risk-management services.

Its scale gives it an unusually broad view of global capital flows. That reach is commercially useful, politically sensitive, and a reminder that passive investing can still concentrate a great deal of operational power in very active hands.

overview.title: Major Products
paragraphs: iShares is BlackRock’s exchange-traded fund platform and one of the company’s most visible product families, giving investors low-cost exposure to equities, fixed income, commodities, factors, and thematic baskets.

Aladdin is BlackRock’s investment operating platform for portfolio management, risk analytics, trading, compliance, and data workflows. It began as internal infrastructure and became a major enterprise product for asset owners, managers, insurers, and other financial institutions.

overview.title: Strategic Position
paragraphs: BlackRock sits at the intersection of asset management, index products, stewardship, financial data, and investment software. Its competitive strength comes from combining distribution, brand trust, product breadth, and embedded institutional workflows.

For the Free The World registry, BlackRock is relevant not only because of consumer-facing fund products like iShares, but because Aladdin represents a deeper layer of financial infrastructure: the tools through which large pools of capital observe, model, and act on markets.

overview.title: Open Alternatives Angle
paragraphs: The most plausible open/free/decentralized comparison areas are portfolio analytics, risk modeling, ETF and fund data access, proxy-voting transparency, and personal or institutional portfolio management tooling.

BlackRock’s products are not simple software subscriptions that can be replaced one-for-one. The registry should separate fund products, fiduciary services, investment data, and workflow platforms so alternatives are evaluated against the actual job being done rather than the mystique generated by very large numbers.

sourceSuggestions.title: BlackRock Investor Relations
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Primary source for annual reports, earnings releases, financial performance, and investor-facing company framing.

sourceSuggestions.title: BlackRock Annual Reports & Proxy
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Best canonical source for business segment language, risk factors, assets under management, strategic priorities, and governance disclosures.

sourceSuggestions.title: BlackRock About Us
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Primary corporate overview for how BlackRock describes its mission, global footprint, client base, and core business lines.

sourceSuggestions.title: BlackRock in the U.S. - About BlackRock
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Useful for retail-facing positioning, iShares references, and plain-language descriptions of BlackRock’s U.S. services.

sourceSuggestions.title: iShares About Us
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Primary source for iShares history, ETF positioning, and BlackRock’s public explanation of the product family.

sourceSuggestions.title: BlackRock Aladdin
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Primary product source for Aladdin’s role as portfolio, risk, trading, compliance, and investment operations infrastructure.

sourceSuggestions.title: BlackRock Investment Stewardship
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Important for assessing BlackRock’s voting, engagement, and governance influence across portfolio companies.

sourceSuggestions.title: CompaniesMarketCap - BlackRock Market Cap
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Useful secondary reference for market-cap tracking and index-ranking context used by the registry queue.
